title: Reply to the Discussion by Lv and Qian of "A Simple Analytical Solution for Organic Contaminant Diffusion Through a Geomembrane to Unsaturated Soil Liner: Considering the Sorption Effect and Robin-Type Boundary" by Lin and Yeh (2020)

research.question: Does the published analytical solution recover the correct organic-contaminant concentration as time approaches infinity?
research.system: Organic-contaminant diffusion through a geomembrane into an unsaturated soil liner with sorption and a Robin-type boundary condition.
research.inputData: The analytical solution published by Lin and Yeh (2020) and the limiting-behavior criticism raised by Lv and Qian; the reply uses no new data.
research.method: The reply checks the infinite-time concentration behavior of the published expression, accepts the writing error, and separately flags a variable-notation correction in the listed equations and discussion line.
research.benchmark: The required infinite-time concentration limit is the sole diagnostic benchmark discussed in the reply.
research.uncertainty: No data, uncertainty analysis, corrected derivation, or replacement concentration curve is provided in this one-page reply.
research.conclusion: The authors accept that the published solution does not obtain the correct concentration at infinite time, identify a writing error, and separately flag a variable correction at the listed source locations.

narrative.throughline: A physically meaningful transport solution must return the correct long-time concentration, and the published expression fails that limit check.
narrative.audienceStartingPoint: The audience understands that contaminant concentration evolves through a layered liner and that an analytical solution must respect its limiting conditions.
narrative.stakes: Using an expression with the wrong infinite-time limit can misrepresent long-term contaminant migration through the landfill liner system.
narrative.resolution: The reply accepts the limiting-behavior criticism, records a separate variable-notation correction at the listed locations, and does not present a revised analytical solution.

background.premise: The reply addresses a published analytical solution for contaminant diffusion through a geomembrane-unsaturated-soil liner system with sorption and a Robin-type boundary.
background.visibleEvidence: A layered geomembrane and unsaturated-soil profile carries one concentration front toward a Robin boundary.
background.whyNow: The original transport object must be visible before testing whether its analytical concentration has the correct long-time behavior.
background.boundary: The reply concerns the specific Lin and Yeh (2020) analytical expression rather than a new transport experiment or a general review of liner models.

background.sourceExcerpt: Reply to the discussion by Lv and Qian of “A simple analytical solution for organic contaminant diffusion through a geomembrane to unsaturated soil liner: Considering the sorption effect and Robin-type boundary” by Lin and Yeh (2020)

methods.transitionBridge: The reported problem folds back onto the original liner profile as a correction flag.

return.claim: The reply accepts that the published solution gives the wrong concentration at infinite time and separately flags a variable correction at the listed equations and line.
return.claimBoundary: Beyond identifying the notation-correction locations, the reply supplies no revised analytical expression or new concentration results.
